title: Docker Compose Skeleton
description: Create the root docker-compose.yml defining all four services (api, mcp, postgres, ollama) with port mappings, named volumes, a shared network, health checks, and dependency ordering. Create .env.example with all required environment variables. Create .gitignore and .dockerignore.
steps: Create docker-compose.yml with services: postgres (image postgres:16, volume pgdata, healthcheck pg_isready), ollama (image ollama/ollama:latest, volume ollama_data, port 11434, healthcheck GET /api/tags validates llama3.2 is present), mcp (build ./mcp_server, port 8001, healthcheck GET /health), api (build . root Dockerfile, port 8000, depends_on postgres+mcp+ollama with condition service_healthy).
Define named volumes: pgdata, ollama_data.

Create .env.example with: OLLAMA_MODEL=llama3.2, OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://ollama:11434, POSTGRES_URL=postgresql+asyncpg://ibge:ibge@postgres:5432/ibge, POSTGRES_USER=ibge, POSTGRES_PASSWORD=ibge, POSTGRES_DB=ibge, MCP_BASE_URL=http://mcp:8001, IBGE_BASE_URL=https://servicodados.ibge.gov.br/api.
Create .gitignore excluding .env, __pycache__, *.pyc, .venv, dist, *.egg-info.
Create .dockerignore excluding .env, __pycache__, *.pyc, .git, tests/, .github/.

title: PostgreSQL Schema Initialization
description: Create the SQL DDL migration file and wire it into the API startup so tables are created automatically when the service starts. Uses SQLAlchemy async ORM with asyncpg driver.
steps: Create src/db/__init__.py (empty).

Create src/db/models.py: define Base = DeclarativeBase(). Define QueryModel (__tablename__='queries') with columns: id (UUID PK, server_default gen_random_uuid()), session_id (UUID nullable), question (Text not null), answer (Text nullable), tools_used (JSONB nullable), latency_ms (Integer nullable), created_at (DateTime with timezone, server_default now()). Define ToolCallModel (__tablename__='tool_calls') with: id (UUID PK), query_id (UUID FK → queries.id, ON DELETE CASCADE), tool_name (Text not null), params (JSONB default {}), result (JSONB nullable), error (Text nullable), called_at (DateTime with timezone, server_default now()).

Create src/db/connection.py: use create_async_engine with POSTGRES_URL from config, define async_session_maker = async_sessionmaker(engine, expire_on_commit=False), export async def get_db() as an async generator yielding AsyncSession for FastAPI Depends.
Create src/config.py using pydantic-settings BaseSettings reading all env vars: ollama_model, ollama_base_url, postgres_url, mcp_base_url, ibge_base_url.
In src/main.py lifespan: call async with engine.begin() as conn: await conn.run_sync(Base.metadata.create_all) to initialize tables on startup.

title: MCP Service Dockerfile and Requirements
description: Create the Dockerfile and pyproject.toml (or requirements.txt) for the mcp_server service. The MCP service runs FastMCP on port 8001.
steps: Create mcp_server/Dockerfile: use python:3.13-slim base, set WORKDIR /app, copy pyproject.toml, run pip install with mcp dependencies (fastmcp, httpx), copy mcp_server/ directory, expose port 8001, set CMD to run the server: python -m mcp_server.server.
Ensure pyproject.toml (at project root, shared) includes mcp_server dependencies: fastmcp>=2.0, httpx>=0.27.
Create mcp_server/__init__.py (empty).
Create mcp_server/tools/__init__.py (empty).

title: API Service Dockerfile and Requirements
description: Create the root Dockerfile for the api service and the pyproject.toml with all Python dependencies for both services. Also create the minimal FastAPI app stub.
steps: Create pyproject.toml with [project] name=ibge-agent, python>=3.13. Dependencies: fastapi>=0.115, uvicorn[standard]>=0.32, langchain>=0.3, langgraph>=0.2, langchain-ollama>=0.2, langchain-mcp-adapters>=0.1, sqlalchemy[asyncio]>=2.0, asyncpg>=0.30, pydantic-settings>=2.0, httpx>=0.27, fastmcp>=2.0. Dev dependencies: pytest>=8, pytest-asyncio>=0.24, ruff>=0.8.
Create root Dockerfile: python:3.13-slim base, WORKDIR /app, copy pyproject.toml, run pip install -e ., copy src/, expose 8000, CMD ["uvicorn", "src.main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"].
Create src/__init__.py (empty).
Create src/main.py: minimal FastAPI app with asynccontextmanager lifespan (stub for now), include a GET / route returning {"status": "ok"}, set app = FastAPI(lifespan=lifespan).

title: MCP Tool: get_states
description: Implement the get_states MCP tool that returns all Brazilian states from the IBGE localidades API. Also create the shared IBGEClient httpx wrapper used by all tools.
steps: Create mcp_server/ibge_client.py: define class IBGEClient with __init__(self, base_url: str) creating an httpx.AsyncClient with 30s timeout. Implement async def get(self, path: str, params: dict | None = None) -> dict | list performing GET requests and returning parsed JSON. Implement async def close(self). Export get_ibge_client() factory.
Create mcp_server/tools/localidades.py.

Implement async def get_states(ibge_client: IBGEClient) -> list[dict]: calls ibge_client.get('/v1/localidades/estados'), returns the list of state objects (each has id, sigla, nome, regiao). Add docstring: 'Returns all 27 Brazilian states with their region information.'
Write unit test in tests/unit/test_tools_localidades.py: mock IBGEClient.get to return fixture data, assert get_states returns list with sigla field.

title: MCP Tool: get_municipalities
description: Implement the get_municipalities MCP tool that returns all municipalities for a given Brazilian state (UF code).
steps: In mcp_server/tools/localidades.py, add async def get_municipalities(uf: str, ibge_client: IBGEClient) -> list[dict]: uppercases uf, calls ibge_client.get(f'/v1/localidades/estados/{uf}/municipios'), returns municipality list. Add docstring: 'Returns all municipalities for a Brazilian state. uf must be a 2-letter state code (e.g. SP, RJ, MG).'
Write test in tests/unit/test_tools_localidades.py: mock IBGEClient.get, assert get_municipalities('sp', ...) calls the correct path with uppercase UF, returns municipality dicts.

title: MCP Tool: get_population
description: Implement the get_population MCP tool that returns Brazil's population by state from the most recent IBGE census via the agregados API.
steps: Create mcp_server/tools/agregados.py.

Implement async def get_population(ibge_client: IBGEClient) -> list[dict]: calls ibge_client.get('/v3/agregados/1705/periodos/-1/variaveis/614', params={'localidades': 'N3[all]'}), returns raw JSON. Add docstring: 'Returns Brazilian population data by state from the most recent IBGE census (aggregate 1705, variable 614).'
Write test in tests/unit/test_tools_agregados.py: mock IBGEClient.get, assert get_population returns correct structure.

title: MCP Tool: search_aggregates
description: Implement the search_aggregates MCP tool that searches IBGE aggregate tables by keyword.
steps: In mcp_server/tools/agregados.py, add async def search_aggregates(keyword: str, ibge_client: IBGEClient) -> list[dict]: calls ibge_client.get('/v3/agregados', params={'pesquisa': keyword}), returns list of matching aggregate tables. Truncate to first 20 results to avoid overwhelming the LLM context. Add docstring: 'Searches IBGE aggregate tables by keyword in Portuguese (e.g. censo, pib, educacao). Returns matching table metadata including id, nome, and pesquisa fields.'
Write test in tests/unit/test_tools_agregados.py: assert search_aggregates passes keyword as query param, returns truncated list.

title: MCP Tool: get_aggregate_data
description: Implement the get_aggregate_data MCP tool that fetches data from a specific IBGE aggregate table by ID, period, and variable.
steps: In mcp_server/tools/agregados.py, add async def get_aggregate_data(aggregate_id: int, period: str, variable: int, ibge_client: IBGEClient) -> list[dict]: calls ibge_client.get(f'/v3/agregados/{aggregate_id}/periodos/{period}/variaveis/{variable}'), returns raw JSON. Add docstring: 'Fetches data from a specific IBGE aggregate table. aggregate_id: numeric table ID from search_aggregates. period: IBGE period syntax like 2022 or -1 (latest). variable: numeric variable ID.'
Write test in tests/unit/test_tools_agregados.py: assert correct URL path is constructed from parameters.

title: MCP Tool: get_name_frequency
description: Implement the get_name_frequency MCP tool that queries name frequency across Brazilian census decades.
steps: Create mcp_server/tools/nomes.py.

Implement async def get_name_frequency(name: str, ibge_client: IBGEClient) -> list[dict]: uppercases name, calls ibge_client.get(f'/v2/censos/nomes/{name.upper()}'), returns frequency distribution list. Add docstring: 'Returns frequency distribution of a Brazilian first name across census decades. name: a single first name in Portuguese (e.g. JOSE, MARIA, ANA). Case-insensitive.'
Write test in tests/unit/test_tools_nomes.py: assert get_name_frequency('jose', ...) calls path with uppercased name /v2/censos/nomes/JOSE, assert lowercase input also works.

title: LangGraph Agent State and Graph Definition
description: Define the LangGraph AgentState TypedDict, system prompt, and assemble the full ReAct StateGraph (START → assistant → tools_condition → tools → assistant loop). Includes the MCP client bootstrap.
steps: Create src/agent/__init__.py, src/agent/state.py, src/agent/prompts.py, src/agent/nodes.py, src/agent/graph.py.
In state.py: define AgentState(TypedDict) with fields: messages: Annotated[list[BaseMessage], add_messages], tools_called: list[dict], question: str, session_id: str | None.

In prompts.py: write SYSTEM_PROMPT instructing the agent to answer questions about Brazil using IBGE tools, cite data sources, respond in the same language as the question, never fabricate statistics.

In nodes.py: define async def agent_node(state, llm_with_tools) that prepends system prompt on first call and invokes the LLM. Define async def tool_node_with_tracking(state, tool_node) that calls ToolNode and extracts tool call records (name, args, result) into tools_called. Define def should_continue(state) returning 'tools' if last message has tool_calls else '__end__'.

In graph.py: define async def build_graph() -> CompiledStateGraph. Load MCP tools via load_mcp_tools(), create ChatOllama(model, base_url), bind tools to LLM. Build StateGraph(AgentState): add agent node (partial with llm_with_tools), add tools node (ToolNode wrapped with tracking), set entry START → agent, add conditional edge agent → should_continue → (tools | END), add edge tools → agent, compile and return. Define async def get_graph() with module-level caching.

title: MCP Client Bootstrap in Agent
description: Implement src/mcp/client.py using langchain-mcp-adapters to connect to the FastMCP server and return LangChain BaseTool instances ready for LangGraph's ToolNode.
steps: Create src/mcp/__init__.py and src/mcp/client.py.
Import MultiServerMCPClient from langchain_mcp_adapters.client.

Define async def load_mcp_tools() -> list[BaseTool]: create MultiServerMCPClient configured with MCP_BASE_URL from config using streamable-http transport at /mcp path, call await client.get_tools(), return tool list.
Add module-level caching: _tools_cache: list | None = None and asyncio.Lock() to ensure load_mcp_tools() only connects once across concurrent calls.
Raise RuntimeError('MCP server unavailable: {detail}') if the connection fails, so the lifespan startup fails fast with a clear message.

title: Tool Call Interception and Persistence
description: Implement src/db/repository.py with save_query() and get_history() functions that persist agent interactions to PostgreSQL.
steps: Create src/db/repository.py.

Implement async def save_query(db: AsyncSession, *, question: str, session_id: UUID | None, answer: str, tools_used: list[dict], latency_ms: int) -> QueryModel: insert QueryModel row, flush to get the generated id, bulk-insert ToolCallModel rows mapping tools_used list (each dict has tool_name, params, result, error) with query_id FK, commit, return the saved QueryModel.

Implement async def get_history(db: AsyncSession, *, session_id: UUID | None = None, limit: int = 20) -> list[QueryModel]: select from QueryModel ordered by created_at DESC, filter by session_id if provided, limit results.

Ensure tool_node_with_tracking in nodes.py extracts tool call records from ToolMessage objects after each tool execution and accumulates them in state['tools_called'] as dicts with keys: tool_name, params (from AIMessage.tool_calls), result (from ToolMessage.content parsed as JSON if possible), error (None on success).

title: POST /chat Endpoint
description: Implement the POST /chat route that accepts a question, invokes the LangGraph agent, persists the result, and returns the answer with tool usage metadata.
steps: Create src/api/__init__.py, src/api/routes/__init__.py, src/api/schemas.py.

In schemas.py: define ChatRequest(BaseModel) with question: str, session_id: str | None = None. Define ChatResponse(BaseModel) with answer: str, tools_used: list[dict], latency_ms: int, query_id: str.
Create src/api/routes/chat.py: implement async def chat(request: ChatRequest, db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)) -> ChatResponse.

Inside handler: record start_time = time.monotonic(), call await get_graph(), invoke with await graph.ainvoke({'messages': [HumanMessage(content=request.question)], 'question': request.question, 'session_id': request.session_id, 'tools_called': []}), extract answer from result['messages'][-1].content, compute latency_ms, call save_query(), return ChatResponse.
Register router in src/main.py: app.include_router(chat_router).
In src/main.py lifespan: call await load_mcp_tools() and await get_graph() at startup to warm up connections before serving traffic.

title: GET /history Endpoint
description: Implement the GET /history route that returns the last 20 queries optionally filtered by session_id.
steps: In src/api/schemas.py: add HistoryItem(BaseModel) with fields: query_id: str, session_id: str | None, question: str, answer: str | None, tools_used: list[dict] | None, latency_ms: int | None, created_at: datetime.

Create src/api/routes/history.py: implement async def get_history_route(session_id: str | None = Query(default=None), limit: int = Query(default=20, le=100), db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)) -> list[HistoryItem].
Call await get_history(db, session_id=UUID(session_id) if session_id else None, limit=limit), map QueryModel objects to HistoryItem, return list.
Register router in src/main.py: app.include_router(history_router).

title: GET /health Endpoint
description: Implement the GET /health route that checks all four downstream dependencies (PostgreSQL, MCP, Ollama, IBGE) and returns a structured health report.
steps: Create src/api/routes/health.py.
Define ServiceStatus(BaseModel) with name: str, ok: bool, detail: str | None. Define HealthResponse(BaseModel) with healthy: bool, services: list[ServiceStatus].

Implement four async check coroutines: check_postgres(db) runs SELECT 1; check_mcp() GET {MCP_BASE_URL}/health expects 200; check_ollama() GET {OLLAMA_BASE_URL}/api/tags expects 200; check_ibge() GET {IBGE_BASE_URL}/v1/localidades/estados expects 200.

In the handler: run all checks with asyncio.gather(*[check_postgres(db), check_mcp(), check_ollama(), check_ibge()], return_exceptions=True). Map results to ServiceStatus list. Set healthy = all(s.ok for s in services). Return HealthResponse.
Register router in src/main.py: app.include_router(health_router).
Write integration test in tests/integration/test_health.py: use FastAPI TestClient, mock all four HTTP checks, test all-healthy case and one-service-down case.

title: GitHub Actions CI Pipeline
description: Create the GitHub Actions CI workflow with three jobs: lint (ruff), unit-tests (pytest tests/unit/), and integration-test (pytest tests/integration/). No Docker services required in CI.
steps: Create .github/workflows/ci.yml.
Set triggers: on push branches [main] and pull_request branches [main].
Job lint: runs-on ubuntu-latest, steps: checkout, setup-python 3.13, pip install ruff, run ruff check src/ mcp_server/ tests/.
Job unit-tests: runs-on ubuntu-latest, needs: [lint], steps: checkout, setup-python 3.13, pip install -e '.[dev]', run pytest tests/unit/ -v.

Job integration-test: runs-on ubuntu-latest, needs: [lint], steps: checkout, setup-python 3.13, pip install -e '.[dev]', run pytest tests/integration/ -v. Note: integration tests use mocked HTTP clients so no Docker services are needed.
Ensure pyproject.toml has [tool.pytest.ini_options] with asyncio_mode = 'auto' and testpaths = ['tests'].
